title: Anthropic Prompt Caching
description: Add Anthropic prompt-caching breakpoints to the AnthropicAdapter so the stable prefix (system prompt + recent message history) is cached across the many multi-turn calls agent-bober makes. This cuts input-token cost on every Anthropic-backed agent run, gated by a providerConfig flag that defaults on for Anthropic and is a no-op for every other provider.
assumptions: The borrow target is agent-bober's Anthropic adapter only; the cockpit (openhands-bober) consumes the rebuilt dist via npm link and needs no change for this feature.
The @anthropic-ai/sdk version in package-lock supports the stable cache_control ephemeral field on system content blocks and message content blocks.
The 'system-and-last-3' breakpoint strategy is the chosen default, capped at Anthropic's maximum of four cache_control breakpoints per request.
Verification is done against a mocked SDK client capturing messages.create arguments; no live Anthropic API call is made in tests.
promptCaching lives as a free-form key inside the existing per-role providerConfig record, so no Zod config-schema migration is required.
outOfScope: Adding caching to the OpenAI, Google, or OpenAI-compat adapters
Extending ChatResponse.usage with cache_read / cache_creation token fields (deferred metrics feature)
Surfacing cache savings in telemetry or the cockpit UI
Changing the default model, max_tokens, tool conversion, or response normalization
Any live-API integration test or cost-measurement harness

features.title: Anthropic prompt caching behind a default-on flag
features.description: The AnthropicAdapter places ephemeral cache_control breakpoints on the system prompt and the most recent message content when prompt caching is enabled, and emits the previous plain-string-system payload when disabled. The factory resolves the flag from per-role providerConfig, defaulting to enabled for Anthropic.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: When promptCaching is enabled, chat() passes the system prompt as a content-block array whose text block carries cache_control of type ephemeral.

AC2: When promptCaching is enabled with two or more messages, a cache_control breakpoint is attached to the latest message content and the request carries at most four cache_control breakpoints total.
AC3: When promptCaching is disabled, chat() sends system as a plain string and no system block, message block, or tool entry carries cache_control.
AC4: createClient reads promptCaching from the role providerConfig, defaults it to enabled for the anthropic provider, and forwards it into the AnthropicAdapter constructor.
AC5: All non-Anthropic provider behavior and the existing provider test suites are preserved, and the package typechecks, lints, and builds.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Enabling prompt caching must reduce repeated input-token billing on multi-turn Anthropic calls by reusing the cached system+prefix, without altering generated output.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test asserts cache_control breakpoints are present on the system block and recent message in the captured messages.create payload when the flag is on.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Disabling the flag must produce a request payload matching the previous plain-string-system shape so the change is fully reversible per call.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test asserts the disabled-path payload contains a string system and zero cache_control fields anywhere in the request.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Caching logic stays confined to the Anthropic adapter and factory wiring; no other provider adapter gains cache_control references.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Grep src/providers for cache_control and confirm matches occur only in anthropic.ts and its test file.

integrationPoints: src/providers/factory.ts — anthropic case (lines ~159-161) resolves promptCaching from providerConfig
src/config/schema.ts — per-role providerConfig is z.record(z.string(), z.unknown()); promptCaching is a free-form key (no migration)
techNotes.dataModel: No persisted data. A boolean promptCaching option threads config -> factory -> adapter; Anthropic system accepts string | TextBlockParam[], and content blocks accept an optional cache_control: { type: 'ephemeral' }.
securityConsiderations: Do not log full cached prompts; preserve existing behavior around API keys and request payloads.
techNotes.existingPatterns: Mirror how OpenAIAdapter already receives providerConfig from the factory (factory.ts:163-168). Follow the existing per-provider .test.ts style (openai.test.ts, google.test.ts) using vitest with a mocked SDK.

metadata.riskNotes: Single-adapter change with a default-on flag and full unit-test coverage via a mocked SDK. Main care points: converting string message content to a content-block array to attach cache_control, and honoring Anthropic's four-breakpoint cap.
